misconceptions.description: Things I got wrong that need correction

entries.context: When adding junit version to gradle/libs.versions.toml
entries.whatIGotWrong: Added version.ref = 'junit' to kotlin-test-junit5, assuming it would use JUnit versioning. Build failed because kotlin-test-junit5:6.0.3 doesn't exist.
entries.correction: kotlin-test-junit5 is released by JetBrains aligned with Kotlin version, not JUnit version. Only junit-platform-launcher should use the JUnit version.

correctMapping.kotlin-test-junit5: uses Kotlin version (2.3.10) - released by JetBrains

entries.context: When build failed after adding junit version
entries.whatIGotWrong: Assumed the protobuf plugin version changes (asProvider().get()) caused the build failure, when actually the failure was from adding version.ref = 'junit' to kotlin-test-junit5
entries.correction: When build fails, verify which change actually caused the failure before attributing blame. The asProvider().get() pattern for protobuf plugin worked correctly.

details.actualCause: Adding version.ref = 'junit' to kotlin-test-junit5 caused artifact lookup failure

entries.whatIGotWrong: Confused about dotfiles structure and symlink setup
entries.correction: The opencode config lives in ~/Code/shell/dotfiles/opencode/.opencode/ NOT directly in ~/Code/shell/dotfiles/opencode/. The install.sh symlinks individual files from .opencode/ subdirectory to ~/.config/opencode/

entries.context: When configuring protobuf plugin artifacts in build.gradle.kts
entries.whatIGotWrong: First tried to use libs.versions.protobuf.get() directly in protobuf plugin block - failed with 'Unresolved reference'
entries.correction: Use libs.versions.<name>.asProvider().get() pattern for protobuf plugin artifact strings. The get() method alone doesn't work in this context because the libs extension isn't available the same way.

details.why: The protobuf plugin's artifact configuration is evaluated in a different context where the libs extension isn't directly accessible. asProvider().get() properly resolves the provider at evaluation time.

entries.context: When making changes to opencode configuration files
entries.whatIGotWrong: Made changes to opencode config files but didn't run install.sh, commit, and push
entries.correction: Always run install.sh to update symlinks, then commit and push changes to dotfiles repo so symlinks remain correct and OpenCode can pick up changes

steps: 4. Commit changes: git add -A && git commit -m 'config: description'
details.critical: Must run install.sh after adding new directories (skills, learnings, etc.) or OpenCode won't see them

entries.context: Before creating a new branch for commits
entries.whatIGotWrong: Created a new branch when already on an appropriate feature branch, leading to unnecessary PR and cleanup
entries.correction: Check git status and current branch before creating commits. If already on a feature branch for the work, commit directly to it instead of creating new branches.

details.correctFlow: git status → already on feat/xyz → commit and push to existing branch
details.wrongFlow: git status → create new branch → commit → push → create PR → merge back to original

entries.context: When version numbers change in version catalogs
entries.whatIGotWrong: Only updated gradle/libs.versions.toml but forgot to update documentation files (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md) that reference the same version
entries.correction: When updating versions in libs.versions.toml, search for and update all documentation files that reference the same version number

details.example: Spring Boot 4.0.3 → 4.0.5 required updates in: libs.versions.toml, AGENTS.md, .claude/CLAUDE.md, buildSrc/AGENTS.md
